channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth scolds NATO allies — again
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasts NATO allies as a "paper tiger" while launching a six-month review of U.S. troop deployments in Europe and pressing for Europe to take the lead on its own defense.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasts NATO allies as a "paper tiger" while launching a six-month review of U.S. troop deployments in Europe and pressing for Europe to take the lead on its own defense.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: In 'Toy Story 5,' tech has come to play
item.description: Three decades after Woody, Buzz Lightyear and pals debuted in Hollywood's first fully computer-animated feature, Pixar brings the whole gang back for a new adventure in Toy Story 5.
item.content:encoded: Three decades after Woody, Buzz Lightyear and pals debuted in Hollywood's first fully computer-animated feature, Pixar brings the whole gang back for a new adventure in Toy Story 5.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Gambling made sports what it is today, according to new book
item.description: Gambling is not something that grew out of American sports. Sports in the U.S. exist in their current form because of gambling. We talk to the author of a new book on sports betting.
item.content:encoded: Gambling is not something that grew out of American sports. Sports in the U.S. exist in their current form because of gambling. We talk to the author of a new book on sports betting.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: Bagpipes, ticker-tape and a whole lot of joy as New Yorkers celebrate the Knicks
item.description: We take you to the big parade in New York City celebrating the New York Knicks, who won their first NBA championship in 53 years.
item.content:encoded: We take you to the big parade in New York City celebrating the New York Knicks, who won their first NBA championship in 53 years.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Mississippi authorities investigate police shooting that left a 1-year-old dead
item.description: Authorities in Mississippi are investigating a police shooting that left a young child dead and an adult seriously injured when police shot at a car in a parking lot during an alleged shoplifting call.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Mississippi are investigating a police shooting that left a young child dead and an adult seriously injured when police shot at a car in a parking lot during an alleged shoplifting call.
item.dc:creator: Elise Gregg

item.title: New Edith Wharton story juxtaposes the horrible with the mundane
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Andrew Gulli, managing editor of The Strand Magazine, about publishing a new short story from Edith Wharton and its resonance today.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Andrew Gulli, managing editor of The Strand Magazine, about publishing a new short story from Edith Wharton and its resonance today.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Handel

item.title: Birders are excited! A brown booby has landed in Kansas City
item.description: A tropical seabird way off course in deeply landlocked Kansas City has set off a birding frenzy. Even as the city hosts the World Cup, for some the most exotic visitor is a Brown booby.
item.content:encoded: A tropical seabird way off course in deeply landlocked Kansas City has set off a birding frenzy. Even as the city hosts the World Cup, for some the most exotic visitor is a Brown booby.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: Sen. Sanders wants Americans to have a say — and stake — in the future of AI
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders talks with NPR's Juana Summers about his new legislation, which would create a sovereign wealth fund, and give the American people a say in regulating AI.
item.content:encoded: Sen. Bernie Sanders talks with NPR's Juana Summers about his new legislation, which would create a sovereign wealth fund, and give the American people a say in regulating AI.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Handel

item.title: 'Diaphanous' crowned the most beautiful English word
item.description: As part of a collaboration between the Poetry Society of America and Babbel, 1,500 poets and poetry lovers weighed in on the most beautiful word in the English language, crowning "diaphanous."
item.content:encoded: As part of a collaboration between the Poetry Society of America and Babbel, 1,500 poets and poetry lovers weighed in on the most beautiful word in the English language, crowning "diaphanous."
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: The DOJ is doling out local police grants — with a catch
item.description: The Justice Department is offering more than a billion dollars to cities and police departments across the U.S., but there's a catch: recipients must cooperate with federal immigration enforcement.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department is offering more than a billion dollars to cities and police departments across the U.S., but there's a catch: recipients must cooperate with federal immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson
